content:
In this guide, we’ll walk through the simple steps to go from a blank page to a fully responsive, animated website.
1. Find Your Inspiration
Every great design starts with an idea. To get started, browse sites like Pinterest, Dribbble, or Landbook.

• Pinterest is great for visual styles, such as “European style 3D relief”.

• Dribbble and Landbook are perfect for finding font and layout inspiration.
2. Create and Refine Your Visuals
Once you have an idea, you need high-quality images. You can use ChatGPT or Figma’s AI features to generate them.

• Pro Tip: You can ask the AI to modify images — for example, adding a “metallic texture” or changing a dark image into a “light style” to match your brand.

• If you want to turn a static image into a video background, you can use a tool like Klingai.com. Simply upload your image, give it a prompt like “animate this,” and you’ll have a professional video ready to host in the cloud.
3. Building with Kimi K3
Now it’s time to head over to Kimi. Make sure you select the Kimi K3 model before you start.
Instead of writing code from scratch, you can use a tool called Motion Sites to find a UI element you like. Copy the prompt for that design, paste it into Kimi, and say “build this”. Within minutes, Kimi K3 will generate a full landing page for you.
4. Customizing Your Site
Kimi K3 makes it incredibly easy to tweak your design:

• Replace Backgrounds: You can drag and drop your generated images or video links directly into the AI and ask it to replace the background,.

• Switch Themes: If the text is hard to read, just ask the AI to “turn the content into a dark mode”.

• Add “Wow” Effects: You can add advanced features like liquid glass effects, parallax scrolling, or a mouse hover reveal effect (where one image transforms into another as you move your mouse over it).
5. Expanding Your Project
One of the best things about Kimi K3 is how fast it learns your style. Once you’ve designed your main page, you can ask the AI to build additional pages — like Services, Pricing, or Download pages — using the same fonts, colors, and animations you’ve already established.
6. Beyond Websites: Building Apps
Kimi K3 isn’t just for websites; it can build functional dashboards and apps.

• For example, you could ask it to build an “ADHD friendly to-do list” with a minimalistic white background.

• If you find a dashboard design you love elsewhere, you can feed that design prompt into Kimi and tell it to “apply this style” to the functional app you just created. It will update the colors and fonts while keeping your app’s structure intact.
Final Thoughts
The most impressive part of using Kimi K3 is that everything it builds is mobile-responsive by default, meaning your site will look great on phones, tablets, and computers without any extra work from you.
What used to take weeks of coding can now be done in a single afternoon. Whether you want to build a portfolio, a client site, or your own productivity app, AI tools like Kimi K3 are turning everyone into a creator. Ready to start building?
